title: IMO reports two commercial vessels attacked in Red Sea within 24 hours near Bab el-Mandeb Strait
summary: The International Maritime Organization reported two separate attacks on commercial shipping vessels in the Red Sea near the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, with one container ship sustaining missile damage and a bulk carrier targeted by drone. No casualties reported.
contents:
# IMO Reports Two Red Sea Shipping Attacks in 24 Hours

## Incident Reports
The International Maritime Organization confirmed two attacks on commercial vessels transiting the Red Sea near the Bab el-Mandeb Strait within the past 24 hours.

## Incident 1: Container Ship
- **Vessel type**: Container ship (Liberian-flagged)
- **Location**: 45nm west of Hodeidah, Yemen
- **Attack method**: Anti-ship missile
- **Damage**: Structural damage to superstructure, vessel proceeding under own power
- **Casualties**: None reported
- **Crew status**: All safe

## Incident 2: Bulk Carrier
- **Vessel type**: Bulk carrier (Marshall Islands-flagged)
- **Location**: 30nm south of Bab el-Mandeb Strait
- **Attack method**: One-way attack drone
- **Damage**: Minor fire extinguished by crew
- **Casualties**: None reported

## IMO Response
- Coordinating with UKMTO and Combined Maritime Forces
- Advisory issued for heightened vigilance in southern Red Sea
- Recommended ships transit at maximum speed through affected area
- IMO Secretary-General condemned attacks as violations of international maritime law

## Shipping Impact
Major container lines reported continued diversions via Cape of Good Hope route, adding 10-14 days to Asia-Europe transit times.
